events.body: Running status against the dbt project. Project parses clean — 18 models, 94 tests, last refresh 06:00 EST.

Running search for 'improper payment', 'recovery', 'office tier', 'vendor match', 'SAM.gov', 'fiscal quarter', 'program code'. Four upstream models surface: fct_improper_payment, fct_vendor_match, dim_program_office, dim_fiscal_calendar.
events.side_effect: tool:search matched fct_improper_payment, fct_vendor_match, dim_program_office, dim_fiscal_calendar

events.body: Running describe on each candidate. fct_improper_payment is payment-grain with payment_date, program_code, office_tier, recovery_status, recovery_amount. fct_vendor_match has sam_entity_status and debarment_flag keyed on payment_id and vendor_id — this is what drives the automated recovery trigger. dim_program_office carries the office_tier classification (1 through 4) and recovery_target_pct. dim_fiscal_calendar provides fiscal_quarter and fiscal_year for the YoY comparison.
events.side_effect: tool:describe returned grain=payment_id on fct_improper_payment · grain=payment_id·vendor_id on fct_vendor_match · grain=program_code on dim_program_office · grain=date_day on dim_fiscal_calendar

events.body: Running lineage. Join path is clean: payments join vendor_match on payment_id, program offices attach via program_code, fiscal calendar attaches via payment_date. Gap confirmed — no model aggregates recovery to (office_tier, program_code, fiscal_quarter). That is exactly what gold.fct_recovery_by_office_tier_program_quarter needs to be.
events.side_effect: tool:lineage validated join path · gap confirmed at tier-program-quarter grain

events.body: Spinning up an XS warehouse and running dbt_show on Q2 FY2026 sliced by office_tier. Tier-3 recovery rate: 61% vs prior-year 79% — an 18-point YoY drop confirmed. Tier-1 recovery rate: 94%, matching target. The data supports the CIO's question exactly. Grain is viable.
events.side_effect: tool:warehouse start XS · tool:dbt_show Q2 slice by office_tier · tier-3 drop 61% vs 79% confirmed · tier-1 94% on target

events.body: Authoring models/gold/fct_recovery_by_office_tier_program_quarter.sql. Writing the config block first — table materialization, gold schema, tags improper_payment and ai_built.
Writing the header docblock so downstream readers know what this model is and who built it.
events.code_append:
-- gold.fct_recovery_by_office_tier_program_quarter
-- Improper-payment recovery rates rolled up to
-- (office_tier, program_code, fiscal_quarter, fiscal_year)
-- Built live by dbt-wizard sub-agents in response to CIO escalation
-- Sources: fct_improper_payment + fct_vendor_match + dim_program_office + dim_fiscal_calendar

events.body: Anchoring the payments CTE with vendor-match join — the sam_entity_status field is what gates automated recovery eligibility. Tier-3 offices route through this feed exclusively.
events.code_append:
with payments_with_vendor as (
    select
        p.payment_id,
        p.payment_date,
        p.program_code,
        p.office_tier,
        p.recovery_status,
        p.recovery_amount,
        v.sam_entity_status,
        v.last_verified_date
    from {{ ref('fct_improper_payment') }} p
    left join {{ ref('fct_vendor_match') }} v
        on v.payment_id = p.payment_id
),

events.body: Joining the fiscal calendar and program office dimensions to get quarter labels and recovery targets.
events.code_append:
with_dimensions as (
    select
        pw.*,
        cal.fiscal_quarter,
        cal.fiscal_year,
        po.office_tier_label,
        po.recovery_target_pct,
        po.bureau_division
    from payments_with_vendor pw
    join {{ ref('dim_fiscal_calendar') }} cal
        on cal.date_day = pw.payment_date
    join {{ ref('dim_program_office') }} po
        on po.program_code = pw.program_code
)

events.body: Final SELECT — one row per (office_tier, program_code, fiscal_quarter, fiscal_year). Payment count, recovery amount, recovery rate, and variance against target.
events.code_append:
select
    office_tier,
    office_tier_label,
    program_code,
    bureau_division,
    fiscal_quarter,
    fiscal_year,
    count(*) as improper_payment_count,
    sum(recovery_amount) as total_recovery_usd,
    avg(case when recovery_status = 'recovered' then 1.0 else 0.0 end)
        as recovery_rate,
    max(recovery_target_pct) as recovery_target_pct,
    avg(case when recovery_status = 'recovered' then 1.0 else 0.0 end)
        - max(recovery_target_pct)
        as recovery_vs_target
from with_dimensions
group by 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6
order by fiscal_year desc, fiscal_quarter desc, office_tier, program_code

events.body: Writing the YAML companion. Description first, then the schema contract — every column gets a type so downstream OIG consumers can resolve it without ambiguity.
events.code_append:
version: 2

models:
  - name: fct_recovery_by_office_tier_program_quarter
    description: |
      Improper-payment recovery rates aggregated to
      (office_tier, program_code, fiscal_quarter, fiscal_year).
      Denominator is all improper payments in the period.
      Built by dbt-wizard sub-agents on 2026-05-23
      in response to CIO escalation on tier-3 Q2 recovery shortfall.
    config:
      contract:
        enforced: true
      tags: [improper_payment, ai_built]
      meta:
        owner: program_integrity_analytics
        built_by: dbt-wizard
        consumes: [fct_improper_payment, fct_vendor_match, dim_program_office, dim_fiscal_calendar]

events.body: Adding column-level tests. Uniqueness on the composite key (office_tier, program_code, fiscal_quarter, fiscal_year). Not-null on recovery_rate and improper_payment_count. Accepted values on office_tier to catch tier taxonomy drift.
events.code_append:
columns:
      - name: office_tier
        data_type: varchar
        tests:
          - not_null
          - accepted_values:
              values: ['1', '2', '3', '4']
      - name: program_code
        data_type: varchar
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: fiscal_quarter
        data_type: varchar
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: fiscal_year
        data_type: number
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: improper_payment_count
        data_type: number
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: total_recovery_usd
        data_type: number
      - name: recovery_rate
        data_type: number
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: recovery_target_pct
        data_type: number
      - name: recovery_vs_target
        data_type: number

    tests:
      - dbt_utils.unique_combination_of_columns:
          combination_of_columns: [office_tier, program_code, fiscal_quarter, fiscal_year]

events.body: Running dbt_show on the compiled model against Q2 FY2026. All column tests resolve clean. Schema contract enforces. Tier-3 recovery rate 61% vs tier-1 94% is visible in the first rows — the 18-point gap the CIO flagged is confirmed in the production data.
events.side_effect: tool:dbt_show compiled · tests:passed · contract:enforced · tier-3 61% vs tier-1 94% confirmed

events.body: Materializing gold.fct_recovery_by_office_tier_program_quarter to Iceberg on GovCloud S3 via the Snowflake dbt adapter. Writing Parquet files to the gold prefix. Registering with the Polaris catalog.

Running lineage one more time. Four upstreams attached. Downstream consumers — OIG briefing dashboard, OMB MAX reporting feed, Treasury IPP reconciliation, Snowflake direct queries — can now resolve the new gold table on their next pass. The CIO's question has a production asset behind it.
events.side_effect: tool:lineage updated · downstream consumers notified · 18 models → 19 models
